advisory.text: infoVan, car, and shuttle service pick-ups and drop-offs may take longer than usual due to construction at JFK. Plan accordingly and allow for extra time due to traffic. We recommend taking Public Transportation to the airport.Learn more

blocks.h2: Getting to and from JFK via shuttle, van, and car service
paragraphs: JFK partners with several trusted van and shuttle services. See our list of authorized providers to reserve your ride.

If you have already booked your ride, go to the ground transportation area to find your driver. If you need to book a ride, contact one of the authorized providers or find an Airport Customer Experience Specialist (ACES) in a red uniform shirt at the Port Authority Information Center in the arrivals area to assist you.
Airlink New York opens in a new tab
ETS Air Shuttle opens in a new tab
Carmel Super Saver opens in a new tab

paragraphs: Uber Shuttle is offered as an alternative, low-cost shuttle service. This is a shared shuttle service that you can book via the Uber app. Shuttle routes are offered both to and from JFK with two routes serving Midtown and Downtown Manhattan.
Learn more about Uber Shuttle routes opens in a new tab

links.text: Learn more about Uber Shuttle routes opens in a new tab

h3s: Uber, Lyft, and other ride apps
Transferring to LaGuardia or Newark Liberty Airport
paragraphs: Uber and Lyft are popular ride share apps available at JFK for your convenience. Ride share apps have designated pickup areas.
Van and shuttle services are one of many options for connecting between airports. Explore all options to get from one airport to another in our transferring airports guide.
Heading to your hotel? JFK offers a designated hotel shuttle pickup and drop-off area. Take the AirTrain to Federal Circle to locate all available hotel shuttles.

links.text: Learn about ride apps at JFK
